message:
perf: optimize scroll handlers and memoize expensive lookups

Improve runtime performance across several components:

- site-header.tsx: Throttle scroll handler using requestAnimationFrame
  to prevent excessive state updates during rapid scrolling. Track
  lastScrolled state to avoid redundant setScrolled calls. Initialize
  scroll state on mount for pages that load mid-scroll. Refactor
  isActive check with early null handling for pathname.

- article-progress.tsx: Cache article metrics (start/end positions) in
  a ref instead of recalculating on every scroll event. Separate
  measurement (expensive DOM reads) from progress calculation. Add
  resize handler that re-measures before recalculating. Guard against
  non-finite scrollRange values.

- flywheel-visualization.tsx: Memoize tool lookup Map for O(1) access
  instead of O(n) array.find() on every render. Extract hasConnection
  helper to simplify and deduplicate connection checking logic.
  Simplify isConnectionHighlighted and isToolConnected callbacks.

- service-worker-registration.tsx: Handle document.readyState === "complete"
  case for immediate registration when useEffect runs after load event.
  Add proper cleanup by removing event listeners on unmount. Extract
  handlers to named functions for correct removeEventListener binding.

These optimizations reduce jank during scroll interactions and improve
overall responsiveness, especially on lower-powered devices.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
